data.keyword: where to buy himalayan salt stone massage kit online

result.keyword: where to buy himalayan salt stone massage kit online

items.markdown:
You can purchase Himalayan salt stone massage kits online from specialized massage supply retailers, natural wellness shops, and major marketplaces. These kits range from professional-grade systems with electronic warmers to budget-friendly sets for home relaxation.[[1]](https://www.massagewarehouse.com/blog/post/discover-the-magic-of-himalayan-salt-stone-massage#:~:text=Himalayan%20sea%20salt%20stones%20are%20not%20just,aside%20from%20the%20traditional%20stone%20massage%20techniques.)[[2]](https://www.ubuy.com.zm/product/I5QMOB32A-qylhh-2-pcs-himalayan-salt-massage-stones-hot-massage-cold-massage-stones-set-warm-rocks-kit-for-facial-spa-3-14-3-14in#:~:text=Himalayan%20Salt%20Massage%20Stones%20Set%20for%20Hot,higher-priced%20salt%20stones%20unsuitable%20for%20their%20needs.)

**Where to Buy Online** 

- **Saltability** : This is a primary source for both professional and starter kits. They offer a [[Himalayan Salt Stone Starter Kit](https://www.google.com/search?ibp=oshop&prds=pvt:hg,pvo:29,imageDocid:1547301468916138496,gpcid:4720473019456455075,headlineOfferDocid:226215969605041834,catalogid:12250368600628725903,productDocid:2563704107221555414&q=product&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwjch9ve-qOTAxWSxwIHHfPvCeAQxa4PegQIBBAD) ](https://www.saltability.com/products/himalayan-salt-stone-starter-kit-for-massage) ($28.95) for basic home use and more advanced [Portable Massage Stone Warmer Sets](https://www.saltability.com/products/portable-massage-stone-warmer-set) ($328.00) that include 15 hand-carved stones and a water-free heater.
- **Massage Warehouse** : Best for professional-grade equipment. They carry the [Saltability Treatment Room Starter Kit](https://www.massagewarehouse.com/products/saltability-himalayan-salt-treatment-room-starter-kit/) ($685.00), which includes 18 stones, a 7-day timer, and a low-energy eco-friendly warmer. They also stock [Harmony Salt sets](https://www.massagewarehouse.com/shop-by-brand/harmony-salt/) ranging from $425.00 to $578.97.
- **Universal Companies** : A reliable retailer for spa-focused kits. They offer [Himalayan Salt Stone Sets](https://www.google.com/search?ibp=oshop&prds=pvt:hg,pvo:29,imageDocid:5821551084977004810,headlineOfferDocid:13384111369677384453,productDocid:13384111369677384453,rds:LO_13384111369677384453%7CPROD_LO_13384111369677384453&q=product&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwjch9ve-qOTAxWSxwIHHfPvCeAQxa4PegQIBBAL) (12 stones for $92.40) and Mobile Massage Sets ($328.89) that feature adjustable thermostats and car adapters for mobile use.
- **Himalayan Secrets** : Offers affordable, home-friendly options like the [Himalayan Salt Massage Stone Warming Bowl](https://www.google.com/search?ibp=oshop&prds=pvt:hg,pvo:29,imageDocid:11554338405162420458,gpcid:8880953961871623319,headlineOfferDocid:5286268971649506125,catalogid:6695514103317111880,productDocid:6830091478451000148&q=product&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwjch9ve-qOTAxWSxwIHHfPvCeAQxa4PegQIBBAO) ($39.95), which includes six stones and uses a heated bowl as the warming mechanism.
- **Etsy (NaturesArtifactsUSA)** : Good for handcrafted, mineral-rich stone sets without electronic warmers. You can find an 11-piece Healing Kit for $49.95 $37.46, which includes various stone shapes and 1 lb of bath salts.
- **Spa & Bodywork Market** : Often has significant discounts on high-end kits, such as the Himalayan Salt Polycarbonate Bowl Warmer with 20 stones for $890.00 $470.00.[[1]](https://www.massagewarehouse.com/shop-by-brand/harmony-salt/#:~:text=Harmony%20Salt%20Himalayan%20Massage%20Salt,Add%20to%20Cart)[[2]](https://www.google.com/search?q=product&prds=pvt:hg,productid:2563704107221555414,catalogid:12250368600628725903,gpcid:4720473019456455075&ibp=oshop)[[3]](https://www.google.com/search?q=product&prds=pvt:hg,productid:3212137914008069883,catalogid:4854331539667885525,gpcid:13453376457847591728&ibp=oshop)[[4]](https://www.google.com/search?q=product&prds=pvt:hg,productid:14276222688709503541&ibp=oshop)[[5]](https://www.google.com/search?q=product&prds=pvt:hg,productid:14276222688709503541&ibp=oshop)[[6]](https://www.google.com/search?q=product&prds=pvt:hg,productid:3552457488345300866&ibp=oshop)[[7]](https://www.google.com/search?q=product&prds=pvt:hg,productid:13384111369677384453&ibp=oshop)[[8]](https://www.google.com/search?q=product&prds=pvt:hg,productid:6830091478451000148,catalogid:6695514103317111880,gpcid:8880953961871623319&ibp=oshop)[[9]](https://www.google.com/search?q=product&prds=pvt:hg,productid:6830091478451000148,catalogid:6695514103317111880,gpcid:8880953961871623319&ibp=oshop)[[10]](https://www.google.com/search?q=product&prds=pvt:hg,productid:9603122939181755113&ibp=oshop)[[11]](https://www.google.com/search?q=product&prds=pvt:hg,productid:10705375335471511083,catalogid:9258518804533844658,gpcid:2542353062234385851,mid:576462866082349749&ibp=oshop)

**Key Considerations** 

- **Warming Method** : Professional kits typically use electronic dry heaters or LED-based warmers to avoid the moisture that can degrade salt stones. Home sets may require you to heat stones in a low-temp oven or use a heated salt bowl.
- **Stone Care** : Himalayan salt is naturally antimicrobial, but stones must be kept away from water. They should be cleaned with a specialized disinfectant like Benefect Decon 30 and can be lightly sanded if they become rough.[[1]](https://www.google.com/search?q=product&prds=pvt:hg,productid:14276222688709503541&ibp=oshop)[[2]](https://www.google.com/search?q=product&prds=pvt:hg,productid:6557729963456802670&ibp=oshop)[[3]](https://www.google.com/search?q=product&prds=pvt:hg,productid:3220795948516962976,catalogid:13384463761870937722,gpcid:5508019206614622784,mid:576462900630954373&ibp=oshop)[[4]](https://www.google.com/search?q=product&prds=pvt:hg,productid:6830091478451000148,catalogid:6695514103317111880,gpcid:8880953961871623319&ibp=oshop)[[5]](https://www.google.com/search?q=product&prds=pvt:hg,productid:6602651611895318402&ibp=oshop)

Are you looking for a **portable** kit for mobile services, or a **stationary** setup for a dedicated home or spa space?
